AceWeather

AceWeather exposes a plain-text weather report endpoint that is intended to be easy for language models to fetch and read.

Preferred endpoint for report text:
- GET /api/report?query=<place name>
- GET /api/report?lat=<latitude>&lon=<longitude>&timezone=<IANA timezone or auto>&label=<human label>

Structured JSON endpoint:
- GET /api/weather?lat=<latitude>&lon=<longitude>&timezone=<IANA timezone or auto>&label=<human label>

Place search endpoint:
- GET /api/search?query=<place name>

Provider status endpoint:
- GET /api/providers

Machine-readable API discovery:
- GET /api
- GET /openapi.json

Examples:
- /api/report?query=Pocklington
- /api/report?lat=53.9093&lon=-0.7810&timezone=Europe/London&label=Pocklington,%20England,%20United%20Kingdom

Notes:
- /api/report returns text/plain.
- /api/weather returns application/json.
- For the simplest workflow, search with /api/search or call /api/report directly with ?query=...
